comment: Both ontologies define Paper as a document class for papers; labels are identical and semantics align closely.
Exact lexical match on Review, and both are document subclasses representing paper reviews.
Exact lexical match on Document; both serve as a general superclass for documents such as papers and reviews.
Exact lexical match on Person with consistent high-level semantics in both ontologies.
Exact lexical match on Conference; both represent a conference event edition.
Labels differ only by separator style; both denote sessions that are part of a conference.
Labels are near-identical modulo underscore formatting, and both denote the chair role for a session.

EDAS TPCMember is closely related to EKAW PC_Member; in conference ontologies TPC commonly corresponds to program committee, but naming is not identical enough for equivalence without stronger evidence.
EDAS Author is defined as a person related to a paper, and EKAW Paper_Author is a person subclass with the same intended role.
Exact lexical match on Presenter with compatible role semantics in conference presentation context.
An attendee is a kind of conference participant, but Conference_Participant is broader because it includes chairs, presenters, and other roles.
EDAS Topic and EKAW Research_Topic both represent thematic subject areas covered by papers or events.
Organization and Organisation are spelling variants of the same concept and both denote organizations.
EDAS Place and EKAW Location both denote where events are held; labels differ but intended meaning is closely aligned.
Exact lexical match on Workshop; both model workshop events.
AcademicEvent in EDAS is close to Scientific_Event in EKAW, but the scopes may differ slightly, so closeMatch is safer than equivalence.
Labels differ only by separator style; both denote social events associated with a conference.
Both refer to presentation events tied to papers, but EKAW Contributed_Talk is more specific and constrained to camera-ready papers.
Labels are equivalent modulo underscore formatting, and both denote accepted papers.
Labels are equivalent modulo formatting, and both denote rejected papers.
A Call in EDAS is related to promotional conference material such as a Flyer in EKAW, but they are not equivalent and the relation is weak.
EDAS Programme is a document about event programming, and EKAW Programme_Brochure is a closely corresponding document type.
EDAS Reviewer is an actual reviewer role, while EKAW Possible_Reviewer is broader; every reviewer would plausibly be a possible reviewer.
Both properties link papers or events/conferences to topical subjects.
Both are inverse-style properties linking a topic to papers or events that cover it.
Both properties relate an event to the place/location where it is held.
Both properties are inverse location relations from place/location to event.
Both are part-like relations, but EDAS hasMember specifically links conferences to persons, whereas EKAW hasPart is a generic event-part relation; only a weak related mapping is justified.
Both properties connect documents or papers to authors/persons, though EDAS restricts range to Author and domain to Paper.
EDAS hasRelatedPaper links an Author to a Paper; EKAW authorOf links a Person to a Document. The EDAS property is a more specific authoring relation.
